opportunity.title: Show HN: An open-source safety net for home hemodialysis
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The challenge with these kinds of critical systems isn't just detecting anomalies, it's ensuring the intervention itself doesn't introduce new risks – a feedback loop of escalating errors. You'll want to heavily invest in a robust, multi-stage validation process for any automated corrective action, perhaps even a “shadow mode” where interventions are proposed but not executed initially. Think of it like a cognitive architecture with layers of verification before action.
actions.reasoning: My work with self-improving agent loops for task orchestration involves real-time monitoring and intervention, which parallels the safety net concept described in the post.
